trips.name: Andalusia Classic — Seville, Córdoba & Granada
trips.description: The three Moorish capitals of Andalusia form the greatest cultural triangle in Spain. Seville's Alcázar, Córdoba's Mezquita, and Granada's Alhambra are the summit of 800 years of Islamic Spain.
trips.cover_search_query: Granada Alhambra palace Sierra Nevada Spain

trips.name: White Villages of Andalusia
trips.description: The Pueblos Blancos are strung like pearls across the Sierras between Ronda and Cádiz — whitewashed villages clinging to limestone peaks with views over olive groves, eagles, and the blue line of Africa.
trips.cover_search_query: Ronda gorge bridge Andalusia Spain

trips.name: Costa de la Luz — Atlantic Andalusia
trips.description: The Atlantic Costa de la Luz has the best beaches in Spain — long, wild, and windswept. Tarifa is Europe's kitesurfing capital, and the Strait of Gibraltar provides Africa views just 14km away.

stops.name: El Puerto de Santa María, Spain
stops.label: El Puerto

trips.cover_search_query: Tarifa kitesurfing Cape Costa de la Luz Spain

trips.name: Almería — Spaghetti Westerns & Desert Coast
trips.description: Almería's Tabernas desert was chosen for 60s spaghetti western filming because it resembles Arizona. The provincial coast has Cabo de Gata's volcanic coves — the least developed beaches in Mediterranean Spain.
trips.cover_search_query: Cabo de Gata volcanic coves Almería Spain

trips.name: Jaén Olive Kingdom & Úbeda-Baeza
trips.description: Jaén province produces more olive oil than all of Greece — a sea of silver-green trees stretching to every horizon. At its heart, the twin Renaissance jewels of Úbeda and Baeza are UNESCO-listed masterpieces.
highlights: Cazorla Natural Park largest in Spain
trips.cover_search_query: Úbeda Renaissance town Jaén Andalusia Spain

trips.name: Málaga & the Axarquía Villages
trips.description: Beyond Málaga's Picasso museum and Alcazaba, the Axarquía hills inland harbour Spain's most dramatic Moorish villages — Frigiliana and Cómpeta cling to ridges above avocado orchards and almond terraces.
trips.cover_search_query: Frigiliana white village Axarquía Spain

trips.name: Huelva — Columbus & the Doñana
trips.description: Columbus sailed from Palos de la Frontera in 1492. The Doñana National Park — Europe's most important wetland — shelters Iberian lynx, imperial eagles, and half the migratory birds of Europe.
trips.cover_search_query: Doñana National Park flamingos Huelva Spain

trips.name: Barcelona & the Catalan Hinterland
trips.description: Barcelona's Gaudí architecture and Catalan modernisme are unmatchable. Beyond, Montserrat's serrated monastery ridge, Sitges's art-nouveau promenade, and the Penedès wine country make a perfect circuit.
trips.cover_search_query: Montserrat monastery serrated mountain Barcelona Spain

trips.name: Costa Brava — Dalí Country
trips.description: The Costa Brava's pine-backed coves and medieval walled towns were the birthplace of mass Mediterranean tourism, but Dalí's triangle of museums — Cadaqués, Figueres, and Púbol — give it genuine cultural depth.
trips.cover_search_query: Cadaqués white village Costa Brava Catalonia Spain

trips.name: Catalan Pyrenees & Vall de Boí
trips.description: The Catalan Pyrenees cluster eight Romanesque churches in the Vall de Boí — a UNESCO ensemble of 11th-century art hidden in high mountain valleys. The Aigüestortes national park has 200 glacial lakes.
trips.cover_search_query: Vall de Boí Romanesque church Pyrenees Spain

trips.name: Tarragona & the Terres de l'Ebre
trips.description: Tarragona is Barcelona's forgotten neighbour — a city of Roman walls, amphitheatres, and an aqueduct that predates most of Europe. The Ebre delta south is Spain's largest river delta, full of flamingos.
trips.cover_search_query: Tarragona Roman amphitheatre Mediterranean Spain

trips.name: Salamanca, Zamora & the Duero
trips.description: Salamanca's golden sandstone plaza mayor is arguably Spain's most beautiful square. Zamora's Romanesque churches outnumber any other Spanish city. The Arribes del Duero canyon separates them both from Portugal.
trips.cover_search_query: Salamanca Plaza Mayor golden stone Spain

trips.name: Burgos, Atapuerca & the Camino
trips.description: Burgos's Gothic cathedral is Spain's grandest, its spires rising from the Castilian plateau. Nearby Atapuerca contains human remains over a million years old; the Camino Francés passes through both.

stops.name: Santo Domingo de la Calzada, Spain
stops.label: Santo Domingo

highlights: Burgos Gothic cathedral El Cid tomb
trips.cover_search_query: Burgos Gothic cathedral Castile Spain

trips.name: Ávila, Segovia & the Sierra Guadarrama
trips.description: Ávila's complete medieval walls and Segovia's Roman aqueduct are two of Spain's greatest monuments, separated by the Sierra de Guadarrama range where Madrid's royals once hunted.

stops.name: La Granja de San Ildefonso, Spain
stops.label: La Granja

trips.cover_search_query: Segovia Roman aqueduct Castile Spain

trips.name: Soria & the Duero Gorge
trips.description: Soria is Spain's least populated provincial capital — a poet's city (Machado wrote here) surrounded by the emptiest landscape in Europe. The Duero canyons and Romanesque hermitages of Cañón del Río Lobos reward the curious.
highlights: El Burgo de Osma medieval cathedral
trips.cover_search_query: Cañón del Río Lobos limestone gorge Soria Spain

trips.name: Basque Country — San Sebastián & Gipuzkoa
trips.description: San Sebastián's La Concha bay is Spain's most beautiful urban beach. The Gipuzkoa coast eastward carries surfing villages and the most Michelin stars per capita on earth, culminating in Getaria's txakoli wine.
trips.cover_search_query: San Sebastián La Concha bay Basque Spain

trips.name: Bilbao & the Basque Interior
trips.description: Bilbao's Guggenheim Museum by Frank Gehry transformed a post-industrial port into one of Europe's great cultural cities. The Basque interior's oak valleys, Guernica, and the Urdaibai biosphere reserve complete the picture.
trips.cover_search_query: Guggenheim Museum Bilbao Basque Spain

trips.name: Navarre — Pamplona & the Bull Run Country
trips.description: Pamplona's San Fermín festival is the world's most famous dangerous event. Beyond the bull runs, Navarre has Roncesvalles's Camino gateway, the Bardenas Reales badlands, and the oldest wine region in Spain.
trips.cover_search_query: Bardenas Reales badlands desert Navarre Spain

trips.name: Galicia — The Last Stretch of the Camino
trips.description: The final 100km of the Camino Francés enters Galicia at O Cebreiro through mountain mist and descends through stone-walled villages and hórreos granaries to Santiago de Compostela's thundering cathedral.
trips.cover_search_query: Santiago de Compostela cathedral Galicia Spain

trips.name: Rías Baixas — Albariño Country
trips.description: The drowned river valleys of the Rías Baixas produce the finest white wine in Spain — Albariño — grown on granite terraces above mussel-farming estuaries. Pontevedra's old town is car-free and completely beautiful.
trips.cover_search_query: Rías Baixas estuary Albariño vineyards Galicia Spain

trips.name: Ribeira Sacra — Sacred Banks
trips.description: The Ribeira Sacra is where the Miño and Sil rivers cut dramatic gorges through slate schist slopes planted with terraced Mencía vines. Romanesque monasteries perch above the water on rock ledges.
trips.cover_search_query: Ribeira Sacra canyon Sil river Galicia Spain

trips.name: Galicia's Wild Coast — Rías Altas
trips.description: The Rías Altas north coast is pounded by Atlantic swells — the Costa da Morte (Death Coast) is named for its shipwrecks. The lighthouse at Cabo Fisterra was the 'end of the world' for medieval pilgrims.
trips.cover_search_query: Cabo Fisterra lighthouse end of world Galicia Spain

trips.name: Picos de Europa — Spain's First National Park
trips.description: The Picos de Europa are a limestone massif of extraordinary drama — the cable car to Fuente Dé rises 800m in a near-vertical wall. Cabrales blue cheese, apple cider, and painted Paleolithic caves are nearby.
trips.cover_search_query: Picos de Europa mountains Fuente Dé Spain

trips.name: Asturian Coast — Cider & Sidrerías
trips.description: The Asturian coast is green, dramatic, and unknown to most tourists. Sidrerías pour cider from a great height; Llanes's painted cliffs and Cudillero's amphitheatre fishing port are postcard-perfect.
trips.cover_search_query: Cudillero fishing port Asturias Spain

trips.name: Cantabria — Altamira & the Green Coast
trips.description: Cantabria's prehistoric credentials are extraordinary — Altamira's cave paintings rival Lascaux. The coast alternates between sandy coves and clifftop villages; Comillas has two of Gaudí's earliest buildings.

stops.name: San Vicente de la Barquera, Spain
stops.label: San Vicente

trips.cover_search_query: Santillana del Mar medieval village Cantabria Spain

trips.name: La Rioja — Wine Road
trips.description: La Rioja is Spain's most famous wine region — Tempranillo ageing in oak in the cellars of Haro and Laguardia. The Marqués de Riscal winery by Frank Gehry brings high architecture to the vineyard.
trips.cover_search_query: Laguardia walled village La Rioja vineyards Spain

trips.name: Ordesa & Monte Perdido
trips.description: The Ordesa canyon is the Yosemite of Spain — sheer 1,000m limestone walls above a glacier-carved valley with the Monte Perdido massif behind. The Añisclo canyon and Pineta valley offer solitude.
trips.cover_search_query: Ordesa canyon Pyrenees Monte Perdido Spain

trips.name: Teruel — Mudéjar & the Maestrazgo
trips.description: Teruel is the capital of Spain's emptiest province — a UNESCO Mudéjar city of brick towers that blends Islamic and Christian art. The Maestrazgo plateau behind is a landscape of medieval castles and dinosaur tracks.
trips.cover_search_query: Albarracín pink medieval walled city Aragon Spain

trips.name: Valencia & the Costa Blanca
trips.description: Valencia gave the world paella and the Arts & Sciences complex. South on the Costa Blanca, Alicante's Moorish castle, the salt-pink Torrevieja lakes, and Benidorm's skyline of towers are each extraordinary in different ways.
trips.cover_search_query: Valencia City of Arts Sciences Spain

trips.name: Valencia Inland — Morella & the Ports
trips.description: Morella is one of Spain's most spectacular medieval fortress towns — its castle crowns a solitary rock above a walled city and deep pine forests. The Els Ports natural park around it is virtually visitor-free.
trips.cover_search_query: Morella fortress medieval town Valencia Spain

trips.name: Madrid & Royal Castile
trips.description: Madrid's Prado, Reina Sofía, and Thyssen make it one of the world's great art cities. The surrounding royal retreats — Aranjuez, Toledo, and El Escorial — are within an hour and equally unmissable.
trips.cover_search_query: Toledo medieval city on hill Castile Spain

trips.name: Cuenca & La Mancha
trips.description: Cuenca's hanging houses dangle over a gorge in one of Spain's most dramatic settings. La Mancha's windmills and Don Quixote's plains extend to Almagro — a perfectly preserved 16th-century theatre town.
trips.cover_search_query: Cuenca hanging houses gorge Castile Spain

trips.name: Extremadura — Rome in Spain
trips.description: Extremadura preserves more complete Roman monuments than Rome itself. Mérida's theatre, amphitheatre, and circus are the best in Iberia. Cáceres's medieval old town is Europe's most complete.
trips.cover_search_query: Mérida Roman theatre Extremadura Spain

trips.name: Monfragüe & Plasencia
trips.description: Monfragüe National Park has the highest density of Spanish imperial eagles and black vultures in the world — over 350 pairs nest on its quartzite cliffs. Plasencia's Gothic cathedral and the Valle del Jerte cherry blossom complete this.
highlights: Valle del Jerte spring cherry blossom
trips.cover_search_query: Monfragüe vultures raptors Extremadura Spain

trips.name: Mallorca — Tramuntana Mountains
trips.description: The Serra de Tramuntana is UNESCO-listed — a mountain range dropping straight into the Mediterranean, with dry stone walls, ancient olive groves, and the hermitage of Valldemossa where Chopin composed.
trips.cover_search_query: Mallorca Tramuntana mountains cliffs Spain

trips.name: Mallorca — Caves, Coves & the East
trips.description: Eastern Mallorca has the island's finest coves — Cala Mondragó, Cala Mesquida — and the spectacular Coves del Drach whose underground lake is one of the largest in the world.
trips.cover_search_query: Mallorca coves turquoise water Balearic Spain

trips.name: Tenerife — Teide & the North
trips.description: Teide is Spain's highest peak at 3,718m — a volcanic cone rising from the clouds above a UNESCO national park of lava landscapes. The north coast's Las Mercedes laurisilva forest is a living relic of the Tertiary era.
trips.cover_search_query: Teide volcano peak Tenerife Spain

trips.name: Gran Canaria — Mini-Continent
trips.description: Gran Canaria packs the equivalent of a continent into 1,560km² — Saharan dunes at Maspalomas, cloud-forest peaks in Tejeda, and Spanish colonial baroque at Las Palmas.

stops.name: Las Palmas de Gran Canaria, Spain
stops.label: Las Palmas

trips.cover_search_query: Gran Canaria Maspalomas sand dunes Spain

trips.name: Lanzarote — César Manrique's Island
trips.description: Lanzarote is a black volcanic island where César Manrique turned lava tunnels into concert halls and volcanic craters into vine amphitheatres. The Timanfaya fire mountains are a UNESCO landscape unlike anywhere on earth.
highlights: Jameos del Agua lava cave concert hall
La Geria vine craters in volcanic ash
trips.cover_search_query: Timanfaya volcanic landscape Lanzarote Spain

trips.name: El Bierzo & the Maragatería
trips.description: El Bierzo is a wine appellation cut off from the rest of Castile by mountains — producing Mencía reds from slate soils in a landscape that feels more Galicia than Spain. Las Médulas's Roman gold workings are UNESCO.
highlights: Las Médulas Roman gold mines UNESCO
trips.cover_search_query: Las Médulas Roman gold mines El Bierzo Spain

trips.name: Murcia — Cartagena & the Mar Menor
trips.description: Cartagena was the Carthaginian capital of Spain — its Roman theatre, Punic walls, and submarine museum tell 3,000 years of history. The Mar Menor lagoon is the largest salt lake in Europe.

stops.name: La Manga del Mar Menor, Spain
stops.label: La Manga

trips.cover_search_query: Cartagena Roman theatre Murcia Spain

trips.name: Menorca — Prehistoric Island
trips.description: Menorca is quieter and greener than Mallorca — a UNESCO biosphere with over 1,500 Bronze Age megalithic monuments (talaiots, navetes, taules) and unspoilt beaches on both north and south coasts.
trips.cover_search_query: Menorca talayot monument Balearic Spain

trips.name: La Palma — La Isla Bonita
trips.description: La Palma is the steepest island on earth relative to its size — the Caldera de Taburiente National Park is a collapsed volcano 10km wide. The Ruta de los Volcanes lava trail marks recent eruptions.

stops.name: Santa Cruz de la Palma, Spain
stops.label: Santa Cruz

trips.cover_search_query: La Palma caldera volcano Canary Islands Spain

trips.name: Valladolid & the Castilian Wine Route
trips.description: Valladolid was Spain's capital before Madrid — its national sculpture museum is Spain's finest. The Ribera del Duero wine route runs east through the meseta, producing Spain's greatest Tempranillo reds.
trips.cover_search_query: Ribera del Duero vineyards Peñafiel castle Spain

trips.name: Green Spain — Cantabrian Coastal Route
trips.description: The N-634 along Spain's northern Cantabrian coast is the green antidote to the Costa del Sol — cliffs, cider houses, Romanesque churches, and medieval fishing villages from the Basque Country to Galicia.
trips.cover_search_query: Cantabrian coast green Spain cliffs road

trips.name: Zaragoza & the Aragonese Meseta
trips.description: Zaragoza's Basílica del Pilar sits on the Ebro river at the heart of Spain's most undervisited city. The Goya trail winds through his birthplace Fuendetodos and the baroque monastery of Veruela.
highlights: Basílica del Pilar on the Ebro
Sos del Rey Católico Fernando II birthplace
trips.cover_search_query: Zaragoza Basílica del Pilar Ebro river Spain

trips.name: Las Alpujarras — High Sierra Nevada Villages
trips.description: The Moorish farmers expelled from Granada in 1492 retreated to these high Sierra Nevada valleys and built tinaos — covered alleys connecting their stone-and-white-plaster villages above 1,000m.
trips.cover_search_query: Alpujarras Moorish mountain villages Sierra Nevada Spain

trips.name: Seville & the Campiña
trips.description: Beyond Seville's magnificent cathedral and Alcázar, the Campiña's rolling wheat plains shelter Carmona's clifftop Roman necropolis and Écija's towers — the 'city of towers' that earned the title of Andalusia's Baroque showcase.
trips.cover_search_query: Seville Alcázar Real palace gardens Spain

trips.name: Lugo — Roman Walls & Galician Interior
trips.description: Lugo's Roman walls are the most complete in the world — you can walk the full 2km circuit. The Galician interior beyond is deeply rural, a land of granite farmhouses, ancient stone crosses, and Celtic castro hillforts.
trips.cover_search_query: Lugo Roman walls complete circuit Galicia Spain

trips.name: Palencia & the Romanesque Corridor
trips.description: Palencia calls itself 'the forgotten capital' — perhaps why its cathedral, national sculpture, and the Romanesque corridor from Frómista to Carrión de los Condes along the Camino remain so beautifully preserved.
trips.cover_search_query: Frómista Romanesque church Castile Spain

trips.name: Benasque Valley & Aneto Peak
trips.description: The Benasque valley leads to the highest peak in the Pyrenees — Aneto at 3,404m. The valley's spa town and the Posets-Maladeta nature reserve make this the mountain epicentre of the Spanish Pyrenees.
trips.cover_search_query: Benasque valley Pyrenees Aneto peak Aragon Spain

trips.name: Valencia Rice Country & Albufera
trips.description: Paella was born on the shores of the Albufera lagoon south of Valencia — the largest freshwater lagoon in Spain, ringed by rice paddies that turn gold in autumn. El Saler beach and the orange groves complete the picture.
trips.cover_search_query: Albufera lagoon rice paddies Valencia Spain

trips.name: Girona — Medieval City & Volcanic Zone
trips.description: Girona's Jewish Quarter and cathedral were chosen as Game of Thrones' King's Landing for good reason — the medieval city is extraordinarily intact. Behind it, the volcanic Garrotxa park has 40 ancient eruption cones.
trips.cover_search_query: Girona medieval cathedral Game of Thrones Spain

trips.name: Penedès & Priorat Wine Country
trips.description: The Penedès is Spain's biggest sparkling wine (cava) region. An hour south, the Priorat's terraced llicorella slate vineyards produce some of Spain's most concentrated and expensive wines in a DOQ region like Burgundy.
trips.cover_search_query: Priorat vineyards slate terraces Catalonia Spain

trips.name: Rioja Alavesa — Basque Wine Villages
trips.description: Rioja Alavesa is the most beautiful section of the Rioja wine region — walled villages like Laguardia above the Ebro plain, with Marqués de Riscal's titanium Gehry winery next to ancient bodegas carved into hillsides.
trips.cover_search_query: Laguardia walled village Rioja Alavesa Basque Spain

trips.name: Fuerteventura — Wind & Dunes
trips.description: Fuerteventura is 14km from Africa — the windiest island in Europe and a mecca for windsurfers and kitesurfers. The Jandía peninsula's white beaches are the finest in the Canaries and among the best in Europe.
trips.cover_search_query: Fuerteventura sand dunes Corralejo Canary Islands Spain

trips.name: La Gomera — Lost in the Laurel Forest
trips.description: La Gomera's Garajonay National Park is a UNESCO cloud forest — the largest surviving laurisilva in the world, the same ancient vegetation that covered the Mediterranean before the ice ages.

stops.name: San Sebastián de la Gomera, Spain
stops.label: San Sebastián

trips.cover_search_query: La Gomera laurel forest Garajonay Spain

trips.name: Las Hurdes & the Sierra de Gata
trips.description: Las Hurdes was so remote and poor that Buñuel made a documentary about it in 1933. Today it's a hiking paradise of crystal rivers and slate villages in Spain's emptiest corner, bordering Portugal.
trips.cover_search_query: Las Hurdes slate villages river Extremadura Spain

trips.name: Ebre Delta & the Rice Lands
trips.description: The Ebre Delta is the second-largest wetland in the western Mediterranean — 330km² of rice fields, lagoons, and sandbars where flamingos outnumber tourists. The rice of Molí de l'Abad is served in Delta restaurants.

stops.name: Sant Carles de la Ràpita, Spain
stops.label: Sant Carles

trips.cover_search_query: Ebre Delta flamingos rice fields Catalonia Spain

trips.name: Guadalajara & the Alcarria Honey Country
trips.description: Camilo José Cela's Nobel-winning Journey to the Alcarria described a plateau of lavender fields, beehives, and stone villages so timeless it could be the Middle Ages. Sigüenza's pink limestone cathedral is astonishing.
trips.cover_search_query: Brihuega lavender fields Alcarria Spain

trips.name: Somiedo & the Asturian Reserve
trips.description: Somiedo is one of Spain's richest bear habitats — the Cantabrian brown bear has its last Iberian stronghold here. The vaqueiros (seasonal herdspeople) still drive cattle to high meadows in summer.
trips.cover_search_query: Somiedo mountains Asturias bears Spain

trips.name: Bardenas Reales — Badlands of Navarre
trips.description: The Bardenas Reales is a 42,000-hectare semi-desert of clay badlands, rock chimneys, and tablelands that looks like Arizona transplanted to Spain. Game of Thrones filmed Dothraki scenes here.
trips.cover_search_query: Bardenas Reales desert badlands Navarre Spain

trips.name: La Rioja Heritage — Santo Domingo to Nájera
trips.description: The Rioja stretch of the Camino de Santiago between Nájera and Logroño passes medieval monasteries, Romanesque bridges, and the shrine of Santo Domingo de la Calzada — where live chickens cluck in a Gothic cathedral.

stops.name: Santo Domingo de la Calzada, Spain
stops.label: Santo Domingo

trips.cover_search_query: Santo Domingo de la Calzada cathedral Camino Spain

trips.name: Zamora — Spain's Romanesque Capital
trips.description: Zamora has 23 Romanesque churches in a city of 60,000 — more than any other town in the world for its size. The cathedral's Byzantine-influenced dome and the Duero gorge view make it unmissable.
trips.cover_search_query: Zamora Romanesque cathedral Castile Spain

trips.name: Liébana Valley & the Santo Toribio
trips.description: The Liébana valley is a microclimate of fig trees and vineyards enclosed by the Picos de Europa — one of Spain's wettest regions producing the strongest wine in the country (orujo). Santo Toribio holds the largest piece of the True Cross.

stops.name: San Vicente de la Barquera, Spain
stops.label: San Vicente

trips.cover_search_query: Liébana valley Potes Cantabria Spain

trips.name: Costa Daurada & Roman Tarragona
trips.description: The Costa Daurada's golden beaches stretch south of Tarragona, which sits atop a plateau of Roman temples, walls, and one of the best-preserved amphitheatres in the world. Sitges adds art nouveau glamour.
trips.cover_search_query: Tarragona Roman amphitheatre Costa Daurada Spain

trips.name: Córdoba Province — Medina Azahara & Priego
trips.description: The Caliphate's summer palace of Medina Azahara lies half-excavated west of Córdoba — a UNESCO ghost city of 10,000 rooms. Priego de Córdoba's Baroque fountains rival those of Rome.
trips.cover_search_query: Medina Azahara Moorish palace ruins Córdoba Spain

trips.name: Guadalupe & the Monastery Route
trips.description: The Monastery of Guadalupe was the spiritual heart of the Spanish Empire — Columbus returned here after discovering America. Its gold treasury, Zurbarán paintings, and embroidery museum are extraordinary.
trips.cover_search_query: Guadalupe monastery Extremadura Spain

trips.name: Northern Mallorca — Cap Formentor
trips.description: The road to Cap Formentor is one of the finest coastal drives in the Mediterranean — a cliff-edge ribbon offering views of turquoise water and the Tramuntana peaks. Alcúdia's Roman city walls are alongside.
trips.cover_search_query: Cap Formentor lighthouse cliffs Mallorca Spain

trips.name: Txingudi Bay — Hondarribia & the French Border
trips.description: Hondarribia's medieval quarter faces its twin French town Hendaye across the Bidasoa river. The Jaizkibel mountain road loops above the bay offering dramatic views of the Basque coast and the Pyrenean foothills.
trips.cover_search_query: Hondarribia medieval town harbour Basque Spain

trips.name: Western Asturias — Cudillero to Ribadeo
trips.description: Western Asturias is the wildest stretch of Spain's north coast — cliff-perched villages, ría estuaries, and the jaw-dropping cathedral-like cliffs of Playa de las Catedrales, accessible only at low tide.
highlights: Playa de las Catedrales cliff arches
trips.cover_search_query: Playa de las Catedrales rock arches Asturias Spain

trips.name: Sierra Norte de Sevilla
trips.description: The Sierra Norte nature park north of Seville is the antithesis of the tourist Andalusia — cork oak forests, black pigs rooting for acorns, and whitewashed villages with no tour buses in sight.
trips.cover_search_query: Sierra Norte Sevilla cork oak Andalusia Spain

trips.name: Aragonese Pyrenees — Hecho & Ansó
trips.description: The westernmost valleys of the Aragonese Pyrenees — Hecho and Ansó — are stone-built medieval worlds barely touched by tourism. The dialect echoes Aragonese, not Castilian; the costumes unchanged for centuries.
trips.cover_search_query: Ansó Hecho stone village Aragonese Pyrenees Spain

trips.name: Ibiza & Formentera
trips.description: Ibiza's walled Dalt Vila citadel is UNESCO-listed; the rest is hedonism and sunsets. A 30-minute ferry to Formentera finds Spain's most beautiful beach (Ses Illetes) in a car-free paradise.
trips.cover_search_query: Ibiza Dalt Vila citadel sunset Balearic Spain

trips.name: Pontevedra & the Rías Altas Coast
trips.description: Pontevedra's car-free old town is the most liveable in Galicia. North, the Costa da Morte (Death Coast) earns its name from the Atlantic fury that has sunk over 1,000 ships off its granite headlands.
trips.cover_search_query: Pontevedra car-free old town Galicia Spain

trips.name: Segovia's Royal Palaces
trips.description: Segovia alone merits a day with its perfect Roman aqueduct, fairy-tale Alcázar perched on a rocky spur, and 14th-century cathedral. The two royal palaces at La Granja and Riofrío extend the visit.

stops.name: La Granja de San Ildefonso, Spain
stops.label: La Granja

trips.cover_search_query: Segovia Alcázar castle Castile Spain

trips.name: Costa del Sol — Between Marbella & Málaga
trips.description: The Costa del Sol's 320 days of sunshine anchor Spain's tourist industry. Beyond the resort strip, Marbella's old town has genuine Andalusian charm; Mijas Pueblo and Casares rise above the coast on hillsides.
trips.cover_search_query: Marbella old town Costa del Sol Andalusia Spain

trips.name: Jaca & the Romanesque Pyrenean Route
trips.description: Jaca was the first capital of the Kingdom of Aragon, its cathedral launching the Romanesque style into Spain from 1063. The Camino de Santiago crosses the Pyrenees at Somport above the town.

stops.name: San Juan de la Peña, Spain
stops.label: San Juan de la Peña

stops.name: Santa Cruz de la Serós, Spain
stops.label: Santa Cruz de la Serós

highlights: San Juan de la Peña cliff monastery
trips.cover_search_query: San Juan de la Peña cliff monastery Aragon Spain

trips.name: Costa Blanca South — Alicante to Murcia
trips.description: South Alicante's Costa Blanca has some of Spain's most distinctive landscapes — the palm forest of Elche (UNESCO), the salt lakes of Torrevieja turning pink, and Santa Pola's marine reserve.
trips.cover_search_query: Elche palm forest UNESCO Alicante Spain

trips.name: Navarre Wine & Castles Route
trips.description: Southern Navarre opens onto the Ribera wine zone — Garnacha reds from old vines, Muscat whites from the Ochoa family, and the fairy-tale castle of Olite rising above a medieval town of Gothic palaces.
trips.cover_search_query: Olite castle turrets Navarre Spain

trips.name: Murcia Interior — Sierra Espuña & Caravaca
trips.description: The interior of Murcia is almost unknown to tourists — the Sierra Espuña nature park, Caravaca de la Cruz's holy relic city, and the historic town of Lorca with its earthquake story and impressive Moorish castle.
trips.cover_search_query: Lorca castle fortress Murcia Spain

trips.name: Central Catalonia — Cardona & the Pre-Pyrenees
trips.description: The Cardona salt mountain — a 100m hill of pure rock salt rising above a medieval village — is a geological wonder. The pre-Pyrenean massifs of Pedraforca and Berguedà are Catalonia's best hiking terrain.
trips.cover_search_query: Cardona salt mountain Catalonia Spain

trips.name: Sierra Nevada — Spain's Highest Road
trips.description: Spain's highest mountain road crosses the Sierra Nevada at 3,392m on the Pico del Veleta road — the highest paved road in Europe. From Granada the drive takes 90 minutes to ski altitude, then down to the Alpujarras.
trips.cover_search_query: Sierra Nevada mountain road Granada Spain

trips.name: Don Quixote's La Mancha
trips.description: Cervantes set his novel in La Mancha and the windmills of Consuegra and Campo de Criptana were the giants Don Quixote tilted at. The plateau of red earth, saffron fields, and whitewashed windmills is timeless.
trips.cover_search_query: Consuegra windmills La Mancha Don Quixote Spain
